title: Disaster risk financing term sheet: Debt pause clauses
content: This Term Sheet covers the key terms and conditions of debt pause clauses available from six Multilateral Development Banks and three bilateral creditors.
snippet: This Term Sheet covers the key terms and conditions of debt pause clauses available from six Multilateral Development Banks and three bilateral creditors.

title: Monitoring and Evaluation (M&E) Specialist (Consultant)

title: Hurricane Helene ravaged farmers’ topsoil. They’re still fighting to build it back.
content: When topsoil is washed away, the necessary nutrients for growing go with it. And when topsoil is covered with sand, farmers can’t get to it. Both scenarios can significantly alter the land’s usability.

title: Barcelona Chamber of Commerce

title: Teaching resilience with the Stop Disasters game: a teacher's guide
content: This Teacher’s Pack supports the use of the  Stop Disasters online game as a practical way to introduce disaster risk reduction (DRR) and resilience in the classroom.
snippet: This Teacher’s Pack supports the use of the Stop Disasters online game as a practical way to introduce disaster risk reduction (DRR) and resilience in the classroom.

title: The pollen trap: seasonal allergies are getting worse — this tech could help
content: Climate change is intensifying pollen seasons in the United States, worsening allergies while new technologies like laser-based forest mapping aim to improve local pollen forecasting and public health response.
snippet: Climate change is intensifying pollen seasons in the United States, worsening allergies while new technologies like laser-based forest mapping aim to improve local pollen forecasting and public health

title: What if Texas’ destructive Tax Day flood had centered on inner Houston instead? It’s why cities should plan for the improbable
content: Simulations show that if the Tax Day storm had centered over the Clear Creek area, more than 13,500 properties with homes would have quickly flooded with at least 6 inches of water. Above 6 inches is the danger zone.

title: What Türkiye’s AFAD-DEHAS platform means for earthquake risk reduction
content: Türkiye has developed a new system: the AFAD Earthquake Early Loss and Damage Analysis System (AFAD-DEHAS). AFAD-DEHAS is a web-based platform that allows multiple users to run analyses simultaneously.
snippet: Türkiye has developed a new system: the AFAD Earthquake Early Loss and Damage Analysis System (AFAD-DEHAS). AFAD-DEHAS is a web-based platform that allows multiple users to run analyses simultaneously

title: Nature-based coastal flood protection: Lessons from the Caribbean and the Philippines

title: The Pacific Island Forum
content: The Pacific Islands Forum brings the region together to address pressing issues and challenges, and foster collaboration and cooperation in the pursuit of shared goals.
snippet: The Pacific Islands Forum brings the region together to address pressing issues and challenges, and foster collaboration and cooperation in the pursuit of shared goals.

title: After the storm, what remains is hope
content: Typhoon Kalmaegi caused significant damage to housing, livelihoods, and public services in Gia Lai province. However, what stands out is the strong sense of solidarity within communities.
snippet: Typhoon Kalmaegi caused significant damage to housing, livelihoods, and public services in Gia Lai province. However, what stands out is the strong sense of solidarity within communities.

title: Global evidence on urban resilience and adaptation to flood
content: New global research shows that floods reduce urban economic activity by 4.5% on average, with losses 3.5 times higher in low-income countries. In this context, CSEP will host  “Global Evidence on Urban Resilience and Adaptation to Flood Risk event.

title: The Centre for Social and Economic Progress
content: The Centre for Social and Economic Progress (CSEP) conducts in-depth, policy relevant research and provides evidence-based recommendations to the challenges facing India and the world.
snippet: The Centre for Social and Economic Progress (CSEP) conducts in-depth, policy relevant research and provides evidence-based recommendations to the challenges facing India and the world.

title: Drought could be making antibiotic resistance worse, scientists say
content: Some studies have found that drier regions of the world tend to report higher levels of antibiotic-resistant infections in hospitals, even when taking differences in wealth and healthcare quality into account. However, these studies show correlation.

title: Can forests withstand and recover from wildfires?
content: While many forests have evolved to adapt to wildfires, more frequent and intense fires, plus increased pressures from climate change, are making even resilient forests more vulnerable.
snippet: While many forests have evolved to adapt to wildfires, more frequent and intense fires, plus increased pressures from climate change, are making even resilient forests more vulnerable.

title: Building the market for resilience: A new opportunity for financial institutions
content: Financial institutions can integrate physical climate risk into credit & investment decisions, deploy capital through mainstream debt & equity products, and design innovative instruments such as contingent finance & resilience bonds to grow their business

title: Análisis de genero e inclusión social en la protección financiera y mecanismos de aseguramiento frente al riesgo climático y de desastres en el Perú
content: La evidencia internacional muestra que los impactos del cambio climático y los desastres no son neutrales al género.

title: Gender analysis of climate and disaster risk finance and insurance in The Gambia
content: This report analyses the extent to which gender considerations are currently integrated into Climate and Disaster Risk Finance and Insurance (CDRFI) in The Gambia, identifying key challenges and gaps.
snippet: This report analyses the extent to which gender considerations are currently integrated into Climate and Disaster Risk Finance and Insurance (CDRFI) in The Gambia, identifying key challenges and gaps.

title: Gender analysis of climate and disaster risk finance and insurance in the Philippines
content: This analysis examines how gender is mainstreamed across key Climate and Disaster Risk Finance and Insurance (CDRFI) related policies and programmes in the Philippines.
snippet: This analysis examines how gender is mainstreamed across key Climate and Disaster Risk Finance and Insurance (CDRFI) related policies and programmes in the Philippines.

title: Moving with dignity: Women leading Fiji’s climate adaptation journey
content: This publication brings together voices from the ground and highlights why human mobility in the context of climate change is inherently gendered: social norms and unequal access to resources shape who participates in decision-making.

title: Voluntary and dignified planned relocation in Somaliland
content: This factsheet presents the Daami’s Dignified Relocation project in Hargeisa, Somaliland, which supported the voluntary relocation of households living in a highly flood-prone displacement camp.
snippet: This factsheet presents the Daami’s Dignified Relocation project in Hargeisa, Somaliland, which supported the voluntary relocation of households living in a highly flood-prone displacement camp.

title: Migration data management in the Philippines

snippet: This factsheet presents migration data management in the Philippines, where digitalization and training of local authorities help streamline the collection, review, and analysis of migration data.

title: Disaster displacement planning in Kenya
content: This factsheet presents how Kenyan cities and counties are integrating disaster displacement into disaster risk reduction planning. How can local authorities prepare for disaster displacement?
snippet: This factsheet presents how Kenyan cities and counties are integrating disaster displacement into disaster risk reduction planning. How can local authorities prepare for disaster displacement?

title: Rubbish deaths: Violence and the environment in Durban, South Africa
content: Based on ethnographic fieldwork in Durban, South Africa, this article examines how environmental violence, masculinity, and inequality intersect in the context of the 2022 floods.
snippet: Based on ethnographic fieldwork in Durban, South Africa, this article examines how environmental violence, masculinity, and inequality intersect in the context of the 2022 floods.

title: Ethnos - Journal of Anthropology (Taylor&Francis)
content: Ethnos publishes peer-reviewed papers promoting theoretical, methodological and empirical developments in the discipline of socio-cultural anthropology.
snippet: Ethnos publishes peer-reviewed papers promoting theoretical, methodological and empirical developments in the discipline of socio-cultural anthropology.

title: Contemporary disasters may not kill more women than men: an empirical inquiry into sex-differentiated fatalities in the twenty-first century
content: This study investigates the claim that women are disproportionately more likely to die in disasters by reviewing existing data sources and compiling new datasets on sex-differentiated disaster fatalities in the twenty-first century.

title: Disaster preparedness capacity development: A scenario-based system for social vulnerability reduction
content: This study highlights gaps in all-hazards disaster preparedness approaches and examines how disaster preparedness planning can systematically draw upon social vulnerability triage.
snippet: This study highlights gaps in all-hazards disaster preparedness approaches and examines how disaster preparedness planning can systematically draw upon social vulnerability triage.

title: Why climate action matters for healthy longevity
content: Climate change has long been associated with risks such as sea-level rise and biodiversity loss. Now it raises deeper questions of whether future generations will live not only longer lives, but healthier ones

title: Earth Capital Nexus
content: Earth Capital Nexus at the London School of Economics and Political Science is a global hub for analytics on resilience and nature for the finance sector.
snippet: Earth Capital Nexus at the London School of Economics and Political Science is a global hub for analytics on resilience and nature for the finance sector.

title: Study on emergency evacuation behavior in heritage tourism destinations based on agent-based modeling: the case of Gulangyu, China
content: This study examines emergency evacuation dynamics in Gulangyu, China, a UNESCO World Heritage site, using agent-based modelling combined with field experiments and behavioural data.
snippet: This study examines emergency evacuation dynamics in Gulangyu, China, a UNESCO World Heritage site, using agent-based modelling combined with field experiments and behavioural data.

title: Concordia study finds park design affects cooling differently by day and night
content: Urban parks are often seen as natural refuges from summer heat, but Concordia research shows that, depending on the time of day, the way trees are arranged within parks can influence whether those spaces cool people down or trap heat.

title: Saltwater is closing in on coastal groundwater, putting billions and food supplies at risk
content: Coastal groundwater is a key source of drinking water in many regions of the world. However, it is threatened by overabstraction and the potential for salinization.
snippet: Coastal groundwater is a key source of drinking water in many regions of the world. However, it is threatened by overabstraction and the potential for salinization.

title: This simple metal tube helps scientists predict drought before it happens
content: A simple century-old tool, the Church Sampler, remains essential for measuring mountain snowpack and predicting water availability, helping scientists anticipate drought risks that are intensifying due to climate change in the western United States.
snippet: A simple century-old tool, the Church Sampler, remains essential for measuring mountain snowpack and predicting water availability, helping scientists anticipate drought risks that are intensifying du

title: Before the wave: gaps in tsunami preparedness
content: The article examines how gaps in tsunami early warning systems and urban evacuation planning—especially for non-seismic tsunamis—can significantly increase risk and fatalities despite existing disaster preparedness measures.
snippet: The article examines how gaps in tsunami early warning systems and urban evacuation planning—especially for non-seismic tsunamis—can significantly increase risk and fatalities despite existing disaste

title: Heat prevention plans help save lives
content: Heat prevention plans across Europe are already saving thousands of lives during extreme heat, leading to a reduction of about 25% in excess deaths attributable to extreme heat.
snippet: Heat prevention plans across Europe are already saving thousands of lives during extreme heat, leading to a reduction of about 25% in excess deaths attributable to extreme heat.

title: Wealth and land-cover change govern landslide fatalities on world’s mountains
content: A new paper Fidan et al. (2026) demonstrates that wealth and the rate of land-cover change play a key role in determining the occurrence of fatal landslides in mountain areas.
snippet: A new paper Fidan et al. (2026) demonstrates that wealth and the rate of land-cover change play a key role in determining the occurrence of fatal landslides in mountain areas.
